name: sbc-validator-operator-coverage
description: Post-v0.16.1 forward plan for SBC Validator. The engine, validators (A-G + S), 4 parsers, signed rules, air-gapped container, and a green release are done and reviewed (see docs/REVIEW-FINDINGS.md). This PRD consolidates the remaining work into two themes: (1) OPERATOR UX so a non-technical user can run it, not just view it; (2) COVERAGE depth gated on a real config. Shipped-this-sprint stories are recorded as done for an honest progress read.
verification: sbc-validator serve   # then load the dashboard, check console is clean

userStories.title: Honest nav scaffolding (no dead links)
userStories.description: The dashboard sidebar had 10 href="#" dead links that read as a wireframe to a technical reviewer. Convert the unbuilt items to visible 'planned' scaffolding.
acceptanceCriteria: Fleet Overview stays the live active page
Unbuilt items are muted, non-clickable (no href), each with a 'soon' pill + 'Planned' tooltip
Dashboard console is clean (no 404s); charts still render

userStories.title: One-command launch + location-independent demo
userStories.description: Global ~/bin launcher + Desktop double-click that open the dashboard with zero setup; demo works from any directory (chdir to repo so cert PEM paths resolve).
acceptanceCriteria: sbc-validator runs from any directory via ~/bin shim
Double-click SBC Validator.command opens the dashboard, auto-picks a free port
demo produces identical verdicts from any cwd (ribbon REVIEW 30 everywhere)

userStories.title: Deep self-audit fixes (review-hardening)
userStories.description: 5-agent deep review closed every acute demo-killer and security finding; shipped as green v0.16.1. See docs/REVIEW-FINDINGS.md.
acceptanceCriteria: 100 tests pass; demo verdicts unchanged
simulate/explain/trust-store/XSS/freshness/air-gap/ACL/NAT/HA/pcap/cert findings closed
v0.16.1 release green incl. air-gapped image smoke

userStories.title: Drag-and-drop validate in the dashboard (C-suite operability)
userStories.description: Turn the read-only dashboard into something a non-technical user OPERATES: drop a config file onto Fleet Overview and see the verdict, without a terminal. This is the Tier-2 unlock that makes it self-serve.
acceptanceCriteria: A drop zone on Fleet Overview accepts a config file
serve.py gains a loopback-only POST /validate that runs the engine in-process and returns the report JSON
Raw config is validated in memory and never written to disk; nothing leaves 127.0.0.1
The verdict + findings render inline (reuse the existing report renderer); errors show a clean message, no traceback
A test covers the /validate endpoint (good config + malformed config)

userStories.title: First real sidebar sub-page: Findings drill-down
userStories.description: Convert one 'soon' nav item into a live page so the sidebar starts earning its chrome. Findings is the highest-value: a fleet-wide, filterable findings table.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Findings shows a table of every finding across the fleet (SBC, domain, severity, check_id, title)
Filter by severity and domain; click a row to jump to that SBC
The 'soon' pill is removed from Findings; it becomes an active nav item
All values html-escaped (same discipline as the fleet table)

userStories.title: Acquire one real sanitized config per vendor (the unlock)
userStories.description: External input from Dico/Philip: a real, sanitized export per vendor (AudioCodes Wizard .ini, Cisco running-config, Ribbon show-config, Oracle ACLI). This is the #1 dependency for COV-002/003. See docs/CONFIG-REQUEST.md.
acceptanceCriteria: One real sanitized config per vendor lands in samples/real/ (gitignored or scrubbed)
The AudioCodes table parser is validated against a real Wizard .ini
Findings from the real config are reviewed against ground truth

userStories.title: Parser honesty for Cisco/Ribbon/Oracle (emit None, not guessed False)
userStories.description: Finish the C1 tristate work for the non-AudioCodes parsers: set SRTP/keepalive/mtls/chain only when observed, leave None otherwise, so a real config never false-fires. Gated on COV-001 to validate against ground truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Cisco/Ribbon/Oracle parsers emit None for unobserved SRTP/keepalive/mtls/chain_complete
No false C.SRTP.DISABLED / B.SIP.OPTIONS_KEEPALIVE / C.TLS.MTLS_DISABLED on a real clean config
Per-vendor silence test (clean real config -> zero false 'disabled' findings)

userStories.title: Per-config cipher-suite + min-TLS assertion (H5)
userStories.description: Close the 'unasserted crypto specs' gap: assert the config actually offers TLS 1.2+ and the required SRTP/cipher suites the ruleset already carries.
acceptanceCriteria: TlsContext gains min_tls_version + offered cipher/SRTP suites (tristate, silent on None)
Domain C asserts TLS-version and suite overlap, driven from the ruleset allowlist
Fires only when the config carries the data; silent otherwise; tests for both
